source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in quadrilateral $ABCD$, a circle $\odot O$ with diameter $AB$ exactly passes through point $C$, and $AC$ and $DO$ intersect at point $E$. It is known that $AC$ bisects $\angle BAD$, $\angle ADC=90^\circ$, and $CD:BC=2:\sqrt{5}$. What is the value of $CE:AE$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} As shown in the figure, connect OC\\
$\because$ AB is the diameter of the circle,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle$ACB=$\angle$ADC=90$^\circ$,\\
$\because$ AC bisects $\angle$BAD,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle$DAC=$\angle$CAB, $\angle$DAB=2$\angle$CAB,\\
$\therefore$ $\triangle ADC\sim \triangle ACB$,\\
$\therefore$ $\frac{CD}{BC}=\frac{AC}{AB}=\frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}=\frac{AD}{AC}$,\\
$\therefore$ $AC=\frac{2\sqrt{5}}{5}AB$,\\
$\therefore$ $BC=\sqrt{AB^{2}-AC^{2}}=\frac{\sqrt{5}}{5}AB$, $AD=\frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}AC=\frac{4}{5}AB$\\
$\therefore$ $CD=\frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}BC=\frac{2}{5}AB$,\\
Also $\because$ $\angle$BOC=2$\angle$CAB,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle$BOC=$\angle$DAB,\\
$\therefore$ AD$\parallel$OC,\\
$\therefore$ $\triangle OCE\sim \triangle DAE$,\\
$\therefore$ $\frac{CE}{AE}=\frac{OC}{DA}=\frac{\frac{1}{2}AB}{\frac{4}{5}AB}=\boxed{\frac{5}{8}}$,\\

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: As shown in the figure, in quadrilateral $ABCD$, a circle $\odot O$ with diameter $AB$ passes through point $C$, and $AC$ and $DO$ intersect at point $E$. It is known that $AC$ bisects $\angle BAD$, $\angle ADC = 90^\circ$, and $CD:BC = 2:\sqrt{5}$. What is the value of $CE:AE$?
normalization_record.normalized_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} As shown in the figure, connect $OC$.\\
$\because$ $AB$ is the diameter of the circle,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle ACB = \angle ADC = 90^\circ$,\\
$\because$ $AC$ bisects $\angle BAD$,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle DAC = \angle CAB$, and $\angle DAB = 2\angle CAB$,\\
$\therefore$ $\triangle ADC \sim \triangle ACB$,\\
$\therefore$ $\frac{CD}{BC} = \frac{AC}{AB} = \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}} = \frac{AD}{AC}$,\\
$\therefore$ $AC = \frac{2\sqrt{5}}{5}AB$,\\
$\therefore$ $BC = \sqrt{AB^{2}-AC^{2}} = \frac{\sqrt{5}}{5}AB$, and $AD = \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}AC = \frac{4}{5}AB$.\\
$\therefore$ $CD = \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}BC = \frac{2}{5}AB$,\\
Also, $\because$ $\angle BOC = 2\angle CAB$,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle BOC = \angle DAB$,\\
$\therefore$ $AD \parallel OC$,\\
$\therefore$ $\triangle OCE \sim \triangle DAE$,\\
$\therefore$ $\frac{CE}{AE} = \frac{OC}{DA} = \frac{\frac{1}{2}AB}{\frac{4}{5}AB} = \boxed{\frac{5}{8}}$.
normalization_notes: Question explicitly references a figure and geometric relations in the diagram.

\textbf{Solution:} As shown in the figure, connect $OC$.\\
$\because$ $AB$ is the diameter of the circle,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle ACB = \angle ADC = 90^\circ$,\\
$\because$ $AC$ bisects $\angle BAD$,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle DAC = \angle CAB$, and $\angle DAB = 2\angle CAB$,\\
$\therefore$ $\triangle ADC \sim \triangle ACB$,\\
$\therefore$ $\frac{CD}{BC} = \frac{AC}{AB} = \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}} = \frac{AD}{AC}$,\\
$\therefore$ $AC = \frac{2\sqrt{5}}{5}AB$,\\
$\therefore$ $BC = \sqrt{AB^{2}-AC^{2}} = \frac{\sqrt{5}}{5}AB$, and $AD = \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}AC = \frac{4}{5}AB$.\\
$\therefore$ $CD = \frac{2}{\sqrt{5}}BC = \frac{2}{5}AB$,\\
Also, $\because$ $\angle BOC = 2\angle CAB$,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle BOC = \angle DAB$,\\
$\therefore$ $AD \parallel OC$,\\
$\therefore$ $\triangle OCE \sim \triangle DAE$,\\
$\therefore$ $\frac{CE}{AE} = \frac{OC}{DA} = \frac{\frac{1}{2}AB}{\frac{4}{5}AB} = \boxed{\frac{5}{8}}$.
